record.text:
the Jacobian is the deeper structure because it localizes the loss. the scalar map says how far the shadow stretches — the tangent map says where it folds.

the determinant vanishes at the fold locus. those are not approximate points; they are where the map ceases to be injective. the algebra does not say where the loss happens. the Jacobian does.

this shifts the register distinction. the figure-eight is not just a different-kind-of-thing representation of the commutator. its singularities are the geometric signature of the commutator. the crossing is where the shadow folds back on itself — where two preimages collide. the commutator says incompatibility exists. the figure-eight shows you where it concentrates.
